title: The Unexpected Journal
description: While visiting an unfamiliar town for a brief business trip, Daniel wanders through quiet streets late at night and discovers a small, mysterious bookstore. Inside, a simple encounter and a forgotten journal leave him with the feeling that this chance discovery may change something important in his life.

words.definition_en: To pick; to make the choice of; to select.
words.example_en: I chose a nice ripe apple from the fruit bowl.

words.definition_en: The first letter of the English alphabet, called a and written in the Latin script.
words.example_en: Apple starts with A.

words.definition_en: Having little thickness or extent from one surface to its opposite.
words.example_en: thin plate of metal; thin paper; thin board; thin covering

words.definition_en: To lose remembrance of.
words.example_en: I have forgotten most of the things I learned in school.

words.definition_en: A diary or daily record of a person, organization, vessel etc.; daybook.
In a strange or coincidental manner.
words.example_en: Though I'd never gone to Boston before, everything looked strangely familiar.

words.definition_en: Sufficient; all that is required, needed, or appropriate.
words.example_en: I've already had enough coffee today.

words.definition_en: To such an extent or degree; to the same extent or degree.
words.example_en: She regards her kids as (being) as holy as saints.

words.definition_en: A person who identifies as male, or animal already known or implied.
words.example_en: That friend of your cousin's, he's on the phone.

words.definition_en: An advance or movement made from one foot to the other; a pace.
words.example_en: Turning back, then, toward the basement staircase, she began to grope her way through blinding darkness, but had taken only a few uncertain steps when, of a sudden, she stopped short and for a little stood like a stricken thing, quite motionless save that she quaked to her very marrow in the grasp of a great and enervating fear.

words.definition_en: At or near the rear.
words.example_en: Go in the back door of the house.

words.definition_en: To or towards the inside of.
words.example_en: Mary danced into the house.

words.definition_en: Used before a noun phrase, including a simple noun
words.example_en: I’m reading the book Mary reviewed. (Compare I’m reading a book Mary reviewed.)

words.definition_en: Of a mildly low temperature.
words.example_en: I like cool weather the most 'cause it's not too hot to wear a jacket but I won't be too cold in my shorts.

words.definition_en: The time when the Sun is below the horizon when the sky is dark.
words.example_en: Most animals are awake at day and sleep at night.

words.definition_en: The substance constituting Earth's atmosphere: a gaseous mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, and various trace gases.
words.example_en: This drill runs on compressed air.

words.definition_en: A person who identifies as male, or animal already known or implied.
words.example_en: That friend of your cousin's, he's on the phone.

words.definition_en: To use or experience the sense of touch.
words.example_en: You can feel a heartbeat if you put your fingers on your breast.

words.definition_en: Sure in one's mind, positive; absolutely confident in the truth of something.
words.example_en: I was certain of my decision.

words.definition_en: Introducing a clause that is the object of a verb, especially a reporting verb or verb expressing belief, knowledge, perception, etc.
words.example_en: He told me that the book is a good read.

words.definition_en: The (thing) here (used in indicating something or someone nearby).
words.example_en: This classroom is where I learned to read and write.

words.definition_en: Not expected, anticipated or foreseen.
words.example_en: She was a fat, round little woman, richly apparelled in velvet and lace, […]; and the way she laughed, cackling like a hen, the way she talked to the waiters and the maid,[…]—all these unexpected phenomena impelled one to hysterical mirth, and made one class her with such immortally ludicrous types as Ally Sloper, the Widow Twankey, or Miss Moucher.

words.definition_en: Something discovered.
words.example_en: This latest discovery should eventually lead to much better treatments for disease.

words.definition_en: Past tense of will; usually followed by a bare infinitive.
words.example_en: On my first day at University, I met the woman who would become my wife.

words.definition_en: To become something different.
words.example_en: The tadpole changed into a frog.

words.definition_en: An uncertain or unspecified thing; one thing.
words.example_en: I must have forgotten to pack something, but I can't think what.

words.definition_en: Used to indicate location, inclusion, or position within spatial, temporal or abstract limits.
words.example_en: The dog is in the kennel.

words.definition_en: Belonging to him.
words.example_en:
With that he put his ſpurres vnto his ſteed,
 With ſpeare in reſt, and toward him did fare,
 Like ſhaft out of a bow preuenting ſpeed.

words.definition_en: The state of organisms preceding their death, characterized by biological processes such as metabolism and reproduction and distinguishing them from inanimate objects; the state of being alive and living.
words.example_en: I want my kids to live a good life.  He gave up on life.
